items.title: 'Cybertongue' could prevent 70 million tonnes of milk waste every year

children.content: About a sixth of the world's milk is wasted, over 150 million tonnes every year, but a Canberra company has come up with new technology that could drastically reduce that.

gallery.altText: A line of two-litre milk bottles in a processing facility.

thumbnail.altText: A line of two-litre milk bottles in a processing facility.

items.title: Even kids want gambling ads banned, so why won't the government do it?

children.content: The government's proposed crackdown on gambling ads doesn't go as far as most Australians want. When you run the numbers, it's a win for the betting industry.

image.altText: Hands holding a mobile phone displaying a gambling app

items.title: Knitters find key to serenity during 'party, party, party' snow season

children.content: As the NSW ski season gets underway, Jindabyne's Snowy Stitchers Social Club finds connection and mindfulness in needles and yarn.

image.altText: Rachelle Edwards, wearing a knitted beanie and gloves, standings in front of a snow field.

items.title: Cost of ANU's reputational damage 'in the order of $100 million'

children.content: The interim vice-chancellor of the Australian National University says a string of governance problems has cost the university about $100 million in reputational damage.

gallery.altText: A woman looks directly at the camera.
Big sign saying Australian National University.

thumbnail.altText: A woman looks directly at the camera.
Big sign saying Australian National University.

items.title: Canberra man facing almost 70 sexual assault, violence charges

children.content: Police said multiple women contacted them in late 2025 and early 2026 to accuse the man of sexual assault while he was in relationships with them.

image.altText: A man with his hands cuffed behind his back is led away by a police officer.

items.title: Brumbies set unwanted records as winless Aussie finals run continues

children.content: The ACT Brumbies have been dumped out of the Super Rugby Pacific finals series after setting unwanted club records in Wellington on Friday.

image.altText: Rugby players with hands on hips, looking dejected

items.title: Canberra Liberals say alleged violent threat was a 'misunderstanding'

children.content: The Canberra Liberals have distanced themselves from former leader Leanne Castley's allegation that she was threatened with physical assault, saying the remark related to an "animated discussion" that was quickly resolved.

gallery.altText: Two politicians speak at a press conference.

thumbnail.altText: Two politicians speak at a press conference.

items.title: Will Australia be taken for a ride in the AI and data centre boom?

children.content: More than $150 billion is lined up for data centre development and investment in Australia, but there are questions as to whether the boom will provide benefits beyond a short-term construction boost.

image.altText: A data centre

items.title: Tasmania's police minister apologises for naming Port Arthur gunman

children.content: Felix Ellis has apologised for comments he made during budget estimates about the 1996 Port Arthur massacre in which he named the gunman, with one survivor labelling his comments "hurtful" and "disrespectful".

image.altText: A bearded man seen in profile.

items.title: Struggling and desperate single mum saved by an 'employment mentor'

children.content: Personalised support helped domestic violence survivor and single mother Harsimran get a job, despite significant barriers. It's an approach that could benefit more jobseekers following the federal government's revamp of Workforce Australia.

gallery.altText: Harsimran leaning on a wooden fence on top of a hill with a green landscape behind her.

thumbnail.altText: Harsimran leaning on a wooden fence on top of a hill with a green landscape behind her.

items.title: Ukraine renews attacks on St Petersburg after Putin rejects Zelenskyy  meeting

children.content: A large-scale Ukrainian drone attack targets St Petersburg, with Russia intercepting 376 drones.

image.altText: A plume of black smoke is seen over the port of St. Petersburg, Russia,

items.title: India's 'cockroach' political movement spills onto Delhi's streets

children.content: India's largest online youth movement has taken to the streets for the first time, calling for the resignation of the education minister.

image.altText: Abhijeet Dipke, head of the Cockroach Janta Party (CJP), speaks to supporters of the CJP

items.title: Tired of streaming? Here's why many are returning to DVDs, VHS and vinyl

children.content: CDs, DVDs, Blue-Rays and even VHS cassettes are enjoying a resurgence. Why some are returning to physical media?

items.title: Diver dies after being bitten by 4.5m shark off Albany, WA

children.content: A male diver has died after an attack by a suspected 4.5-metre shark near Michaelmas Island, off WA's Albany coast, on Saturday morning.

image.altText: police albany 3

items.title: Yengi scores on debut as Socceroos draw with Switzerland in WC warm-up

children.content: The Socceroos respond after a poor first half to claim a 1-1 draw against Switzerland in their final match before the FIFA World Cup.

image.altText: Tete Yengi of Australia celebrates with teammates after scoring a goal against Switzerland.

items.title: Planning, packing and nerves: Six Aussies kick off Race Around The World

children.content: Six Australian filmmakers are about to embark on the journey of a lifetime on Race Around The World. Here's how they're preparing for the unknown.

Lucinda poses while somene takes a photo on a camera.

items.title: Mexico score late winner against sloppy Matildas

children.content: The lacklustre Matildas have been beaten by Mexico for the first time in over 20 years, losing 1-0 in Newcastle.

image.altText: Diana Ordonez runs with her arms out

children.content: As the NSW ski season gets underway, Jindabyne's Snowy Stitchers Social Club finds connection and mindfulness in needles and yarn.

items.title: 'We were so close': Laos cave rescue called off as conditions deteriorate

children.content: The search for two men trapped in a flooded cave in Laos has been postponed as rescuers say a "miracle" is needed.

gallery.altText: Rescuers stand under torchlight near the entrance of a cave in Laos

thumbnail.altText: Rescuers stand under torchlight near the entrance of a cave in Laos

items.title: Get a buzz out of spelling? It's time to show off your skills

children.content: Admit it, you can't get enough of my synonyms and similes. Let's get spelling!

image.altText: Guy Montgomery beams as he stands in front of the Guy Mont set in front of a podium bearing a cup, purse and bucket.

items.title: Pope competes with Bad Bunny in Spain, urges leaders to reject polarisation

children.content: Pope Leo urges global leaders not to "fan the flames of polarisation" to gain popularity, warning of the artificial environment created by new technologies.

image.altText: Pope Leo stands behind protective glass in the popemobile as it moved through a crowded street escorted by security

items.title: Death Cafes help people 'make the most of our finite lives'

children.content: The Death Cafe concept began in Switzerland in the 2000s to help people "make the most of our finite lives". Now, the concept has spread globally.

gallery.altText: A graphic of three photos of an older man, one with a younger man and baby.
a sign saying let's talk about death

thumbnail.altText: A graphic of three photos of an older man, one with a younger man and baby.
a sign saying let's talk about death

items.title: Shots fired where gangster's funeral was advertised to be held

children.content: The ABC understands the funeral of Sydney gangster Lorenzo Lemalu was originally scheduled to take place at the venue in Punchbowl, but the plans were changed.

image.altText: a window with a hole after it was shot

items.title: Andrew Moloney claims world title two years after quitting in anger

children.content: Andrew Moloney weathered champion Willibaldo Garcia's best over 12 brutal rounds to win a majority decision in their super flyweight world title clash in Japan.

image.altText: Boxer yells with excitement at the end of a fight, standing in the ring

items.title: 'It's not normal to be winning the way I'm winning': Johnson excelling in open water

children.content: Moesha Johnson is enjoying an overseas open water swimming adventure that has seen her win gold at every stage. But these are all just stepping stones for the biggest prize of all at the Los Angeles Olympics in 2028.

gallery.altText: Moesha Johnson smiles with a medal around her neck
Moesha Johnson punches the air in the water

thumbnail.altText: Moesha Johnson smiles with a medal around her neck
Moesha Johnson punches the air in the water

items.title: Power hang on for thrilling win over Eagles, Dockers claim record win

children.content: Port Adelaide hangs on for a thrilling win over West Coast after Logan Morris kicked seven in a Brisbane QClash win and Fremantle beat North Melbourne by 124 points to claim the biggest win in its history.

image.altText: Port Adelaide players hug and and congratulate each other after a win

items.title: Russian teen Andreeva wins French Open to claim first major title

children.content: Mirra Andreeva defeats surprise finalist Maja Chwalińska 6-3, 6-2 to become the youngest French Open champion in more than three decades.

image.altText: Mirra Andreeva holds the French Open trophy.

items.title: How the corporate world influenced Diamonds' netball coaching

children.content: The world has never been closer to Australia's Diamonds. Coach Stacey Marinkovich is determined to keep them shining brightest.

gallery.altText: Stacey Marinkovich gestures while coaching the Diamonds at the 2022 Commonwealth Games.

thumbnail.altText: Stacey Marinkovich gestures while coaching the Diamonds at the 2022 Commonwealth Games.

items.title: Antonelli snatches Monaco pole with 'magic lap', Piastri off the pace

children.content: Kimi Antonelli makes a mockery of suggestions the Monaco Grand Prix would stall his incredible start to the Formula 1 season, while Oscar Piastri struggles to make an impression.

image.altText: Kim Antonelli with Max Verstappen and Lewis Hamilton

items.title: Broncos' season in crisis as Titans young gun conjures golden moment

children.content: Keano Kini scores one of the great game-winning tries to condemn Brisbane to a fifth-straight defeat, with the Titans snatching a 28-23 victory at Lang Park.

image.altText: A rugby league player in black has his hands on his head and is looking glum.

items.title: Father of infant boy charged with grievous bodily harm

children.content: A man is charged after his nine-week-old baby was taken to hospital with life-threatening injuries in April.

image.altText: The front of the new hospital.

items.title: Private audience with pope given to small-town parish delegates

children.content: A priest and two parishioners from the Queensland town of Childers meet Pope Leo XIV in Vatican City and have a wooden cross blessed.

image.altText: The pope dressed in white robes, shakes hands with a priest dressed in black robes

items.title: 30 years of Pokémon: The 'immersive' phenomenon still finding new fans

children.content: Three decades after the franchise that coined the phrase "Gotta catch 'em all!" was first released, parents are sharing their love of the franchise with their children.
